did_this_paper_use_ai_to_obtain_results_.details: The paper is a review study that summarizes recent advances in the diagnosis of skin lesions using dermoscopic images based on deep learning. It discusses the application of deep learning (DL) and artificial intelligence (AI) for melanoma classification, skin lesion segmentation, and feature extraction, analyzing various DL models, frameworks (e.g., Keras, PyTorch), and datasets (e.g., ISIC) used in dermatology.

is_this_paper_a_review_paper_or_a_resear.details: The abstract explicitly states 'This review study focuses on the state-of-the-art automatic diagnosis of skin cancer from dermoscopic images based on deep learning.' The index terms include 'literature review,' and Section I.C describes a literature search methodology using Web of Science to summarize existing research, datasets, and models rather than presenting new experimental results.

non_compliance_reason: The paper is a review, survey, or meta-analysis rather than an original research paper.
